app.title: Rebar Editing UI — MIDAS Gen NX
app.subtitle: MIDAS Gen NX · Powered by Open API — anyone can connect with their own MAPI Key and edit their own Gen NX model.
app.footerHint: Dimension, spacing, and cover values follow the active length unit currently used in the MIDAS model. Section previews are schematic and may differ from the actual layout/spacing (beam/column/brace use the center section, wall uses the plan section).
lang.label: Language
conn.title: Connection Settings
conn.mapiKeyPlaceholder: Copy from Gen NX → API Settings
conn.baseUrlPlaceholder: Leave blank to use the MIDASIT cloud API (recommended)
conn.hint: Gen NX must be running with API status set to Connect.
common.dimsHintTitle: Dimensions for diagram display only (not saved, preview only)
common.loadedSectionInfoUnnamed: Loaded element {key} · no section name found
common.tableHint: Edit multiple loaded elements on one screen; each row saves independently.
common.bulkSaveHint: Saving will apply the same rebar to all {count} elements using this section: {keys}
beam.modeEach: I / M / J separately (Each End & Center)
beam.modeHint: Most beams use "Same for whole span" or "Ends + Center" for constructability. "I / M / J separately" is only for beams that genuinely need distinct rebar at each station.
beam.modeChangeConfirm: The sector(s) hidden by this mode currently have different values — switching will overwrite them to match what's shown. Continue?
beam.memberPreviewHint: Pick one element sharing this section to check its actual saved values. Saving still applies the values shown here to every element in the section.
beam.checkHint: Computes phiMn/phiVn instantly from the diagram dimensions (B/H) and cover (DT/DB) above. This is a singly-reinforced approximation (compression steel ignored); use the button below to pull Mu/Vu from your Gen NX design results, or enter them manually.
beam.checkUnsupported: This design code isn't supported yet (only KDS 41 20:2022 and TWN-USD112)
beam.fetchHint: Only works for a member whose design check (BC-ANAL) has already been run in Gen NX. You can still edit the values after loading.
beam.fetchEmpty: This member hasn't been checked in Gen NX yet (BC-ANAL not run).
wall.createSub: Create Sub Wall ID (specify story range)
wall.segmentHint: This wall has {count} segments. Only the selected one is edited — the others are kept unchanged on save.
js.connDisconnected: The key is valid, but Gen NX is not connected. Turn on Gen NX and enable the API.
js.connMismatch: This key is for the {program} product. Please check your product selection.
js.parseError: Could not parse the server response.
js.noItems: No registered items (you can enter a new key directly and save).
js.keyExists: That element number is already in the list — edit it directly in its row above.
js.saveBulkPartialFail: {failed} of {total} failed to save (elements: {keys}) — {error}. The rest were already applied to Gen NX.
js.saveDone: Applied to the Gen NX model.
js.svgWallPartial: (only part of the actual length is shown)
js.err.timeout: Gen NX is taking too long to respond (timed out). Please try again shortly.
project.title: Project Review
project.hint: Reads basic info from the currently open Gen NX model: element counts, sections, materials, load combinations, and boundary conditions.
project.colConstraint: Constraint (DX DY DZ RX RY RZ RW)
geo3d.title: 3D Geometry View
geo3d.hint: Renders the model's nodes and members (built from /db/NODE + /db/ELEM; columns/beams/braces are inferred from member orientation, walls from plate elements).
geo3d.sceneLoadError: Something went wrong rendering the 3D view.
guide.title: User Guide
guide.intro: This tool connects directly to the MIDAS Gen NX Open API to edit rebar. If this is your first time, follow the steps below.
guide.step1: Open Gen NX → get a MAPI Key from the top menu's API Settings and copy it.
guide.step2: Click the "API Settings" button in the top-right, paste the MAPI Key, choose the product (Gen NX/Civil NX), then verify the connection and save.
guide.step3: Select the design code and rebar material standard at the top (standards verified against real Gen NX data are marked with a badge).
guide.step4: In the Beam/Column/Wall/Brace tabs, pick the member to edit and enter the rebar values (use the dropdown for a quick pick of rebar sizes). Saving applies the change directly to your Gen NX model.
guide.step5: Use the Project Review tab to see a full model summary (elements/sections/materials/load combinations/boundary conditions) and a 3D geometry view.
guide.step6: In the "Design Check" panel at the bottom of the Beam tab, enter fck/fy and Mu/Vu (from your Gen NX design results) to see phiMn/phiVn and a Check Ratio instantly (KDS 41 20:2022 and TWN-USD112 only; compression steel is ignored as an approximation).
designCode.hint: The rebar sizes offered in the BEAM/COLUMN/WALL/BRACE input dropdowns follow the design code picked here.
designCode.overrideHint: Material standard differs from this design code's default (e.g. Eurocode design with Korean rebar).
guide.codeConfirmWarning: This design code's material mapping has regional variation — please double-check for your actual project.
guide.release.d260723.item1: Added a quick-pick rebar size dropdown to every bar-name field (supports D/#/Ø/M notations)
guide.release.d260723.item2: Design code and rebar material standard can now be selected independently, with a verified badge on standards checked against real Gen NX data
guide.release.d260723.item3: Reworked the connection settings into an "API Settings" button with a status indicator
guide.release.d260723.item4: Rebuilt the 3D geometry view on React Three Fiber (fixed walls not rendering, nodes now hidden by default and shown on click)
guide.release.d260723.item5: The existing-member dropdown now shows each section's name alongside its number
guide.release.d260723.item6: Cover-thickness fields now show the model's actual length unit
guide.release.d260723.item7: Added an instant phiMn/phiVn design-check panel to the Beam tab (KDS 41 20:2022, TWN-USD112)
guide.release.d260722.item1: Fixed BEAM rebar saving to match the real Gen NX API's write field format (verified against a live model)
guide.release.d260722.item2: Rebuilt the entire app in TypeScript + Vite + React
guide.release.d260722.item3: Added a planner credit next to the developer byline
guide.release.d260722.item4: Base URL now defaults to the correct address for the selected product
guide.release.d260722.item5: Added a Project Review tab: model summary (elements/sections/materials/load combinations/boundary conditions) plus a 3D geometry view
guide.release.d260722.item6: Populated the code reference table with 37 design codes across 12 countries and their linked rebar material standards
guide.release.d260716.item1: Initial MIDAS Gen NX rebar-editing UI (Beam/Column/Wall/Brace)
guide.release.d260716.item2: Reworked into a Vercel-deployable app with serverless API functions
guide.release.d260716.item3: Added multilingual UI support (English/Korean/Simplified Chinese/Traditional Chinese)
guide.release.d260716.item4: Added IP-based automatic language detection and a developer byline
